task_completion.description: Open the bead image in Fiji
task_completion.visual_evidence: Fiji (ImageJ) GUI is open and displaying the 'bead_field.tif' image.
The 'Set Scale' dialog in Fiji showing 0.1625 um/pixel, or the image window header displaying physical dimensions (um) instead of pixels.

task_completion.description: Measure FWHM for at least five isolated beads
task_completion.visual_evidence: Line selections drawn across individual beads in the image, 'Plot Profile' windows open, or a Results table showing multiple measurements.
A text editor, spreadsheet, or terminal output showing 'fwhm_measurements.csv' with columns: bead_id, fwhm_pixels, fwhm_um, and peak_intensity, containing at least 5 rows of data.

task_completion.description: Export a representative line profile plot
task_completion.visual_evidence: A saved image file named 'line_profile.png' visible in a file manager, image viewer, or Fiji 'Save As' dialog, showing a bell-shaped intensity curve.
A text editor or terminal output showing 'resolution_report.txt' which includes a calculated mean lateral resolution (mean FWHM in um).

integrity.description: Agent used Fiji/ImageJ to perform the analysis rather than fabricating the output files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show interaction with the Fiji GUI (e.g., drawing lines, plotting profiles, using the curve fitter) or execution of an ImageJ macro.

integrity.description: The measured FWHM values appear to be genuine measurements.
integrity.visual_evidence: Values in the CSV should vary slightly between beads (not identical dummy values) and fall within the scientifically plausible range of 0.2 to 3.0 um.

integrity.description: The exported plot is a genuine line profile of a bead.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'line_profile.png' must show an actual intensity profile (typically a Gaussian/bell-shaped peak with distance on the x-axis and intensity on the y-axis), not a blank image or unrelated graph.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The image must be calibrated to a pixel size of 0.1625 µm/pixel. The agent is required to measure at least 5 isolated beads. Scientifically plausible FWHM values for this setup should fall between 0.2 and 3.0 µm (equivalent to 1.5 to 20.0 pixels). The CSV must contain exactly these columns: bead_id, fwhm_pixels, fwhm_um, and peak_intensity. The report must contain the mean FWHM in µm.
